  • Hide All Code

Alports Glom EC Analysis

Jonathan Nelson

09 December, 2025

1 Load Packages

if (!require("knitr")) {install.packages("knitr"); require("knitr")}
if (!requireNamespace('BiocManager', quietly = TRUE)) {install.packages('BiocManager'); require("BiocManager")}
if (!require("dplyr")) {install.packages("dplyr"); require("dplyr")}
if (!require("stringr")) {install.packages("stringr"); require("stringr")}
if (!require("Seurat")) {install.packages("Seurat"); require("Seurat")}
if (!require("sctransform")) {install.packages("sctransform"); require("sctransform")}
if (!require("glmGamPoi")) {BiocManager::install('glmGamPoi'); require("glmGamPoi")}
if (!require("patchwork")) {install.packages("patchwork"); require("patchwork")}
if (!require("ggplot2")) {install.packages("ggplot2"); require("ggplot2")}
if (!require("EnhancedVolcano")) {BiocManager::install('EnhancedVolcano'); require("EnhancedVolcano")}
if (!require("DESeq2")) {BiocManager::install('DESeq2'); require("DESeq2")}
if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse"); require("tidyverse")}
if (!require("RColorBrewer")) {install.packages("RColorBrewer"); require("RColorBrewer")}
if (!require("car")) {install.packages("car"); require("car")}
if (!require("openxlsx")) {install.packages("openxlsx"); require("openxlsx")}
if (!require("readxl")) {install.packages("readxl"); require("readxl")}
if (!require("ggrepel")) {install.packages("ggrepel"); require("ggrepel")}
if (!require("gghighlight")) {install.packages("gghighlight"); require("gghighlight")}
if (!require("ggpmisc")) {install.packages("ggpmisc"); require("ggpmisc")}
if (!require("data.table")) {install.packages("data.table"); require("data.table")}
if (!require("here")) {install.packages("here"); require("here")}
if (!require("clusterProfiler")) {BiocManager::install('clusterProfiler'); require("clusterProfiler")}
if (!require("enrichplot")) {BiocManager::install('enrichplot'); require("enrichplot")}
if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse"); require("tidyverse")} # for data frame transformation
if (!require("gplots")) {install.packages("gplots"); require("gplots")}
if (!require("ggvenn")) {install.packages("ggvenn"); require("ggvenn")}
if (!require("ggrepel")) {install.packages("ggrepel"); require("ggrepel")}

library("EnhancedVolcano")
library(UpSetR)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
#install.packages("ggtext")
library(ggtext)
library(kableExtra) 


options(future.globals.maxSize = 74 * 1024^3) # 55 GB
getOption("future.globals.maxSize") #59055800320
## [1] 79456894976
here()
## [1] "F:/KRC Datasets/Sargis/alport_gloms"

2 Introduction

2.1 Email from Sargis on 11/3/2025

Q1: The color codes assigned to GEC subtypes in the heatmap do not match those used in the bar graphs showing subtype frequences. I was able to classify the subtypes based on the top 5 markers in the heatmap but was not able to compare between the groups due to this issue.

Q2: EC subtype classification: Please, let me know what you think about it.

Q3: Could you please help me identify the genes in these Venn diagrams, (also including the comparison between 1EV/AS vs 4M/WT) especially those overlapping. I tried to extract them from the excel files using different cut offs, but I kept getting different numbers than yours. Could not figure out why. And then it would be interesting to do pathway analysis (or GO analysis) on these sets of genes for each Venn diagram.

Q4: Would it be possible to have a pseudobulk PCA analysis of the groups? like for examples WT, 4M, 1EV and 2EV in one PCA plot, and separately WT, 4M AS and 6m AS in a different PCA plot?

SO <- readRDS(file = here("outputs", "Alports_Glom_EC.rds"))

# DimPlot(SO, label = T)

3 Q1

3.0.1 Creating a Consistent Color Palette v1

library(scales)   # ggplot2 depends on this

# ggplot2 default discrete palette (6 colors)
my_colors <- scales::hue_pal()(7)

# my_colors

type_levels <- c(
  "Type_A",
  "Type_B",
  "Type_C",
  "Type_D",
  "Type_E",
  "Type_Mito",
  "Type_Prolif"
)

names(my_colors) <- type_levels
#my_colors
SO <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(SO)

SO.markers <- FindAllMarkers(SO, only.pos = TRUE, min.pct = .25)

SO.markers %>%
    group_by(cluster) %>%
    dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 1) %>%
    slice_head(n = 5) %>%
    ungroup() -> top10

# DoHeatmap(SO, features = top10$gene) + NoLegend()

3.1 Rename v1

SO2 <- SO

SO2@meta.data <- SO2@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(subclass_EC = dplyr::case_when(
    seurat_clusters == 0  ~ "Type_A",
    seurat_clusters == 1  ~ "Type_A",
    seurat_clusters == 2  ~ "Type_B",
    seurat_clusters == 3  ~ "Type_B",
    seurat_clusters == 4  ~ "Type_C",
    seurat_clusters == 5  ~ "Type_D",
    seurat_clusters == 6  ~ "Type_Mito",
    seurat_clusters == 7  ~ "Type_D",
    seurat_clusters == 8  ~ "Type_E",
    seurat_clusters == 9  ~ "Type_Prolif"
  ))

SO2@meta.data$subclass_EC <- factor(
  SO2@meta.data$subclass_EC,
  levels = c(
    "Type_A",
    "Type_B",
    "Type_C",
    "Type_D",
    "Type_E",
    "Type_Mito",
    "Type_Prolif"
  )
)

3.1.1 Creating a Consistent Color Palette v1

library(scales)   # ggplot2 depends on this

# ggplot2 default discrete palette (6 colors)
my_colors <- scales::hue_pal()(7)

# my_colors

type_levels <- c(
  "Type_A",
  "Type_B",
  "Type_C",
  "Type_D",
  "Type_E",
  "Type_Mito",
  "Type_Prolif"
)

names(my_colors) <- type_levels
#my_colors
DimPlot(SO2, group.by = "subclass_EC",) + scale_color_manual(values = my_colors)

3.2 Rename v2

3.2.1 Creating a Consistent Color Palette v2

library(scales)   # ggplot2 depends on this

# ggplot2 default discrete palette (6 colors)
my_colors2 <- scales::hue_pal()(7)

# my_colors

type_levels <- c(
  "Type_1",
  "Type_2",
  "Type_3",
  "Type_4",
  "Type_5",
  "Type_6",
  "Type_7"
)

names(my_colors2) <- type_levels
my_colors2
##    Type_1    Type_2    Type_3    Type_4    Type_5    Type_6    Type_7 
## "#F8766D" "#C49A00" "#53B400" "#00C094" "#00B6EB" "#A58AFF" "#FB61D7"
SO2@meta.data <- SO2@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(subclass_EC_2 = dplyr::case_when(
    subclass_EC == "Type_A"  ~ "Type_1",
    subclass_EC == "Type_B"  ~ "Type_2",
    subclass_EC == "Type_C"  ~ "Type_4",
    subclass_EC == "Type_D"  ~ "Type_3",
    subclass_EC == "Type_E" ~ "Type_6",
    subclass_EC == "Type_Mito"  ~ "Type_5",
    subclass_EC == "Type_Prolif"  ~ "Type_7"
  ))

SO2@meta.data$subclass_EC_2 <- factor(
  SO2@meta.data$subclass_EC_2,
  levels = c(
    "Type_1",
    "Type_2",
    "Type_3",
    "Type_4",
    "Type_5",
    "Type_6",
    "Type_7"
  )
)

DimPlot(SO2, group.by = "subclass_EC_2") + scale_color_manual(values = my_colors2)

3.3 Table of EC Cell Type Identities

Cell Clusters with Marker Genes and Cell States
Cluster Markers Cell_State
Type_1 Csrnp1, Nfkbiz, Ppp1r10, Socs1, Dusp5 Immune-regulatory
Type_2 Ddnrb, Fabp4, Gda, Akap12, Pde7b Lipid, structural maintenance, quiescent
Type_3 Slc6a6, Pltp, Atp13a3, Aqp1, Slc26a10 Transport-focused
Type_4 Slc45a4, Gja5, Esm1, Cxcr4, Hey1 Angiogenic
Type_5 mt-Nd5, mt-Nd4, mt-Nd3, mt-Nd2, mt-Nd4l Mitochondrial-rich
Type_6 Dlk1, Rtl1, Sez6l2, Egln3 Developmental
Type_7 Uhrf1, Gins2, Mki67, Fignl1, Rad51 Proliferative

3.4 Heatmap

t1 <- table(SO2@meta.data$subclass_EC_2, SO2$group)[, c("WT", "4M_Alp", "4M_Alp_1EV", "4M_Alp_2EV", "6M_Alp")]
#t1

prop.t1 <- prop.table(t1, margin = 2) 
#prop.t1

t2 <- as.data.frame(t1)
colnames(t2) <- c('Cell_type', 'Group', 'Frequency')

# Original plot

ggplot(t2, aes(fill=Cell_type, y=Frequency, x=Group)) + 
  geom_bar(position="fill", stat = "identity", fun.y = "mean", colour="black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors2) +
  theme_classic()

4 Q2

Seems good to me and see above for changing the names of the EC clusters and creating a consistnent color palette.

5 Q3

5.1 The Problem

Part of the problem you were facing in trying to compare the Venn Diagrams and the CSV DEG lists were created in different ways and from different files at different points in the analysis.

DEG lists:

From Alports_Quantify_Number_of_DEGs_WTv4M_Alp_1EV_09182025

Using SCT assay and Seurat_5.3.0

for (i in clusters) { DefaultAssay(SO) <- “SCT” DEGs <- FindMarkers(SO, ident.1 = “4M_Alp_1EV”, ident.2 = “WT”, group.by = “group” , subset.ident = i, logfc.threshold = .1, min.pct = .1) DEGs <- DEGs %>% rownames_to_column(“gene”) %>% filter(p_val_adj < .05) DEGs <- DEGs[order(DEGs[,“avg_log2FC”], decreasing = TRUE),] assign(paste0(“Alports_4M_”,names(clusters[i])), DEGs, envir = DEG.env)

Venn Diagram: Alports_subset_ECs_Quantify_Reversal_Genes_0528202

I think using RNA assay and Seurat_4.4.0

df1 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_A, ident.2 = Condition_Baseline, group.by = “group”, subset.ident = “EC_glom”, logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

5.2 The Solution

Start with the DEG lists from Alports_Quantify_Number_of_DEGs_WTv4M_Alp_1EV_09182025 and create Venn Diagrams and new intersection lists to perform Pathways Analysis.

5.3 Update: Solution Didn’t work

5.3.1 The Previous DEG lists aren’t the correct comparisons for making the Venn Diagrams

5.3.2 They were really setup to look at all the different populations…not just ECs

5.3.3 Solution #2

Recreate the DEG lists again from the Alports_Glom_EC.rds dataset and save them as csv files as well.

5.3.4 Re-create DEG Lists

df1 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_A, ident.2 = Condition_Baseline, group.by = “group”, subset.ident = “EC_glom”, logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df2 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_B, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = “group”, subset.ident = “EC_glom”, logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df3 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_C, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = “group”, subset.ident = “EC_glom”, logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df4 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_D, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = “group”, subset.ident = “EC_glom”, logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

5.3.4.1 DEG Setup

Condition_Baseline <- "WT"

Condition_A <- "4M_Alp"
  
Condition_B <- "4M_Alp_1EV"

Condition_C <- "4M_Alp_2EV"

Condition_D <- "6M_Alp"

clusters <- unique(SO2@meta.data$class_merge)
names(clusters) <- clusters

SO <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(SO2)
DEG.env <- new.env()

Idents(SO2) <- SO2@meta.data$group

DefaultAssay(SO2) <- "SCT"

5.3.4.2 df1

4M_Alp vs. WT

DEGs <- FindMarkers(SO2, ident.1 = Condition_A, ident.2 = Condition_Baseline, logfc.threshold = .1, min.pct = .1)
DEGs <- DEGs %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>% filter(p_val_adj < .05)
DEGs <- DEGs[order(DEGs[,"avg_log2FC"], decreasing = TRUE),]

#remove the first column of from the dataframe DEGs
DEGs <- rownames_to_column(DEGs)
DEGs <- DEGs[,-1]

df <- head(DEGs, 20)

df
df1 <- DEGs

5.3.4.3 df2

4M_Alp_1EV vs. 4M_Alp

DEGs <- FindMarkers(SO2, ident.1 = Condition_B, ident.2 = Condition_A, logfc.threshold = .1, min.pct = .1)
DEGs <- DEGs %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>% filter(p_val_adj < .05)
DEGs <- DEGs[order(DEGs[,"avg_log2FC"], decreasing = TRUE),]

#remove the first column of from the dataframe DEGs
DEGs <- rownames_to_column(DEGs)
DEGs <- DEGs[,-1]

df <- head(DEGs, 20)

df
df2 <- DEGs

5.3.4.4 df3

4M_Alp_2EV vs. 4M_Alp

DEGs <- FindMarkers(SO2, ident.1 = Condition_C, ident.2 = Condition_A, logfc.threshold = .1, min.pct = .1)
DEGs <- DEGs %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>% filter(p_val_adj < .05)
DEGs <- DEGs[order(DEGs[,"avg_log2FC"], decreasing = TRUE),]

#remove the first column of from the dataframe DEGs
DEGs <- rownames_to_column(DEGs)
DEGs <- DEGs[,-1]

df <- head(DEGs, 20)

df
df3 <- DEGs

5.3.4.5 df4

6M_Alp vs. 4M_Alp

DEGs <- FindMarkers(SO2, ident.1 = Condition_D, ident.2 = Condition_A, logfc.threshold = .1, min.pct = .1)
DEGs <- DEGs %>% rownames_to_column("gene") %>% filter(p_val_adj < .05)
DEGs <- DEGs[order(DEGs[,"avg_log2FC"], decreasing = TRUE),]

#remove the first column of from the dataframe DEGs
DEGs <- rownames_to_column(DEGs)
DEGs <- DEGs[,-1]

df <- head(DEGs, 20)

df
df4 <- DEGs

5.3.4.6 Filter and Split Positive and Negative Lists

df1_pos <- df1 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df1_pos
df1_neg <- df1 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df1_neg
df2_pos <- df2 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df2_pos
df2_neg <- df2 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df2_neg
df3_pos <- df3 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df3_pos
df3_neg <- df3 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df3_neg
df4_pos <- df4 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df4_pos
df4_neg <- df4 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df4_neg

5.4 Venn Diagram Readout

5.4.1 Up -> Down

5.4.1.1 4M vs 4M_1EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df2_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 1EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = FALSE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_1EV <- test$'A:B'

5.4.1.2 4M vs 4M_2EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df3_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 2EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = FALSE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_2EV <- test$'A:B'

5.4.1.3 4M vs 6M

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df4_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 6M' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = FALSE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_6M_Alp <- test$'A:B'

5.5 Write Intersection Genes

6 Q4

6.1 Pseudobulk PCA

6.1.1 ALL GROUPS

# head(SO2@meta.data)

pbo <- AggregateExpression(SO2, 
                           group.by = "sample",
                           assays = 'RNA', 
                           slot = "counts",
                           return.seurat = FALSE)

pbo <- pbo$RNA
# pbo[1:5, 1:5]
colnames(pbo) <- sub("^g", "", colnames(pbo))
# colnames(pbo)[1:5]

# DimPlot(SO2, group.by = "sample")

metadata <- SO2@meta.data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Alports = names(sort(table(alports), decreasing = TRUE))[1],  # Most common value
    Batch = names(sort(table(batch), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    Group = names(sort(table(group), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

rownames(metadata) <- metadata$sample

# head(metadata)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = round(pbo),
                              colData = metadata,
                              design = ~ sample)

rld <- rlog(dds, blind=TRUE)

sample_names <- unique(rld$sample)

6.1.1.1 PCA By Sample

# Plot PCA
sample<-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "sample") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(sample)

6.1.1.2 PCA By Alports

alports <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Alports") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(alports)

6.1.1.3 PCA By Group

group <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Group") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(group)

6.1.2 No 6M Alports

# head(SO2@meta.data)

pbo <- AggregateExpression(SO2, 
                           group.by = "sample",
                           assays = 'RNA', 
                           slot = "counts",
                           return.seurat = FALSE)

pbo <- pbo$RNA
# pbo[1:5, 1:5]
colnames(pbo) <- sub("^g", "", colnames(pbo))
pbo <- pbo[, !colnames(pbo) %in% c("A", "B", "C")]
#colnames(pbo)

# DimPlot(SO2, group.by = "sample")

metadata <- SO2@meta.data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Alports = names(sort(table(alports), decreasing = TRUE))[1],  # Most common value
    Batch = names(sort(table(batch), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    Group = names(sort(table(group), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

metadata <- metadata %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Group != "6M_Alp")

rownames(metadata) <- metadata$sample

# head(metadata)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = round(pbo),
                              colData = metadata,
                              design = ~ sample)

rld <- rlog(dds, blind=TRUE)

sample_names <- unique(rld$sample)

6.1.2.1 PCA By Sample

# Plot PCA
sample<-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "sample") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(sample)

6.1.2.2 PCA By Alports

alports <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Alports") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(alports)

6.1.2.3 PCA By Group

group <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Group") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(group)

6.1.3 No EVs

# head(SO2@meta.data)

pbo <- AggregateExpression(SO2, 
                           group.by = "sample",
                           assays = 'RNA', 
                           slot = "counts",
                           return.seurat = FALSE)

pbo <- pbo$RNA
# pbo[1:5, 1:5]
colnames(pbo) <- sub("^g", "", colnames(pbo))
pbo <- pbo[, !colnames(pbo) %in% c("473", "375", "364", "368", "475", "362")]
#colnames(pbo)

# DimPlot(SO2, group.by = "sample")

metadata <- SO2@meta.data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Alports = names(sort(table(alports), decreasing = TRUE))[1],  # Most common value
    Batch = names(sort(table(batch), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    Group = names(sort(table(group), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

metadata <- metadata %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Group != "4M_Alp_1EV")

metadata <- metadata %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Group != "4M_Alp_2EV")

rownames(metadata) <- metadata$sample

# head(metadata)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = round(pbo),
                              colData = metadata,
                              design = ~ sample)

rld <- rlog(dds, blind=TRUE)

sample_names <- unique(rld$sample)

6.1.3.1 PCA By Sample

# Plot PCA
sample<-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "sample") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(sample)

6.1.3.2 PCA By Alports

alports <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Alports") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(alports)

6.1.3.3 PCA By Group

group <-DESeq2::plotPCA(rld, ntop = 1000, intgroup = "Group") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample_names)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.5) +
  theme_classic()

plot(group)
